page.title: Games/So I'm a Spider, So What? Ruler of the Labyrinth

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Game |Guid=e9753e16-cf49-4d15-9a52-08ded4b677a3 |Name=So I'm a Spider, So What? Ruler of the Labyrinth |Aliases=Spider RotL |Image=Kumoicon.png |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2024-10-23 |Concepts=Concepts/RPG Elements, Concepts/Survival, Concepts/Isekai |Developers=Companies/CTW |Genres=Genres/MMORPG |Locations=Locations/Labyrinth Zone, Locations/Dungeon |Objects=Objects/G Spider |Platforms=Platforms/Browser |Publishers=Companies/CTW }} ''So I'm a Spider, So What?..."
revision.text: {{Game |Guid=e9753e16-cf49-4d15-9a52-08ded4b677a3 |Name=So I'm a Spider, So What? Ruler of the Labyrinth |Aliases=Spider RotL |Image=Kumoicon.png |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2024-10-23 |Concepts=Concepts/RPG Elements, Concepts/Survival, Concepts/Isekai |Developers=Companies/CTW |Genres=Genres/MMORPG |Locations=Locations/Labyrinth Zone, Locations/Dungeon |Objects=Objects/G Spider |Platforms=Platforms/Browser |Publishers=Companies/CTW }} ''So I'm a Spider, So What? Ruler of the Labyrinth'' is a free-to-play dungeon-clearing role-playing game developed for HTML5 web browsers and distributed worldwide on the [https://g123.jp/?lang=en G123] platform operated by CTW, Inc. The title is the first video game adaptation of the ''So I'm a Spider, So What?'' light novel and anime series, written by Okina Baba and illustrated by Tsukasa Kiryu, with the original light novel series published by KADOKAWA. The game launched on October 23, 2024.

{{Game |Guid=c9389320-9c82-4e69-9a6d-e962737d546d |Name=Star Trek (1971) |Aliases=Star Trek |Image=Star Trek text game.png |Deck=An early text-based strategy game based on Star Trek, wherein the player hunts down Klingon ships with phasers and torpedoes. The goal is to eliminate all Klingon vessels and have the Enterprise survive. |ReleaseDateType=Year |YearPart=1971 |Concepts=Concepts/Text Based, Concepts/Turn Based |Franchises=Franchises/Star Trek }} == Overview == In 1971, Mike Mayfield developed Star Trek after playing [https://giantbomb.com/wiki/Games/Spacewar Spacewar!] in the computer lab at University of California, Irvine. Written on the Sigma 7, Star Trek required use of a teleprinter. After visiting the Hewlett-Packard sales office, Mayfield was offered time on their HP 2000C minicomputer in order to write a new version in HP's version of BASIC, which was released in 1973.
